label: Ukraine launches long-range missile strikes on Russian territory causing casualties and injuries
label_zh: 烏克蘭對俄羅斯領土發動遠程導彈襲擊，造成傷亡

timeline.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Ports and Refineries as Massive Missile Exchanges Escalate Across Regional Borders
timeline.title_zh: 烏克蘭襲擊俄羅斯港口和煉油廠，大規模導彈交換在地區邊境升級
timeline.sub_title: Ukrainian long-range operations hit strategic energy and logistics hubs in Krasnodar while Moscow retaliates with a massive 500-weapon aerial assault targeting Kyiv and other key regions.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 烏克蘭的遠程行動襲擊了克拉斯諾達爾的戰略能源和物流樞紐，而莫斯科則以針對基輔和其他關鍵地區的 500 件大規模空中襲擊進行報復。
timeline.summary:
The conflict between Russia and Ukraine reached a new level of intensity on March 14, 2026, as Ukrainian forces executed a series of long-range strikes against high-value strategic targets deep within Russian territory and occupied regions. The most significant of these operations targeted the Krasnodar Krai region, where Ukrainian deep-strike units successfully hit the Port Kavkaz and the Afipsky oil refinery. Russian authorities confirmed that at least three people were injured during the attack on Port Kavkaz, a critical maritime logistics hub located on the Kerch Strait. The strike reportedly damaged pier infrastructure and a service vessel, with one individual requiring hospitalization. [ref:3] Simultaneously, drone debris at the Afipsky oil refinery—one of the largest in southern Russia—sparked a significant fire, though no immediate casualties were reported at that facility.

In tandem with the Krasnodar strikes, Ukrainian Special Operations Forces deployed long-range drones to target Russian missile systems in occupied Crimea. Reports indicate that at least one Iskander-M operational-tactical missile launcher was destroyed near the village of Vyshneve while it was loaded and prepared for a launch. Footage released by the Ukrainian military showed secondary detonations, suggesting a direct hit on the ammunition. Additionally, a hangar in the settlement of Kurortne, believed to be used for storing further missile hardware, was also struck. These [ref:2] operations are part of a broader Ukrainian campaign aimed at degrading Russia’s ability to conduct its own missile strikes against Ukrainian cities.

In a massive retaliatory response, Russia launched one of its largest coordinated aerial assaults of the year overnight and throughout Saturday. According to Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy, Russian forces deployed approximately 430 attack drones and 68 missiles of various types, including Zircon anti-ship missiles and Iskander-M ballistics. The primary target was the energy infrastructure surrounding the capital, Kyiv, though strikes were also recorded in the Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Kharkiv regions. The assault [ref:5, 1] resulted in at least five deaths and dozens of injuries across Ukraine, causing widespread power outages and significant damage to residential buildings and schools.

This escalation comes amid a heated diplomatic row between the Kremlin and Western powers. On March 13 and 14, the Russian Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the ambassadors of the United Kingdom and France to lodge a formal protest regarding the use of Western-supplied long-range weapons in strikes on Russian soil, specifically citing a previous attack in the Bryansk region that killed seven people. Moscow has accused NATO specialists of providing the direct intelligence and technical support necessary for such precision strikes, warning of a 'fundamentally new level' of destruction if such operations continue. As international attention remains divided by ongoing conflicts in the Middle East, both Moscow and Kyiv appear to be intensifying their deep-strike capabilities in a bid to seize the strategic initiative.
timeline.summary_zh:
2026 年 3 月 14 日，隨著烏克蘭部隊在俄羅斯境內和佔領區深處針對高價值戰略目標發動一系列遠程襲擊，俄羅斯與烏克蘭之間的衝突達到了新的激烈程度。其中最重大的行動目標是克拉斯諾達爾邊疆區，烏克蘭深遠打擊部隊成功襲擊了 Port Kavkaz 和 Afipsky 石油煉油廠。俄羅斯當局證實，Port Kavkaz 襲擊事件中至少有三人受傷，該港口是刻赤海峽重要的海上物流樞紐。據報導，襲擊損壞了碼頭基礎設施和一艘服務船，其中一人需住院治療。同時，Afipsky 石油煉油廠（俄羅斯南部最大的煉油廠之一）的無人機殘骸引發了大火，但該設施沒有立即報告人員傷亡。[ref:3]

與克拉斯諾達爾的襲擊同步，烏克蘭特種作戰部隊動用遠程無人機攻擊了克里米亞佔領區的俄羅斯導彈系統。據報導，至少有一枚 Iskander-M 戰術導彈發射器在 Vyshneve 村附近被摧毀，當時該導彈正在裝載並準備發射。烏克蘭軍方發布的畫面顯示了二次爆炸，表明導彈彈藥受到直接命中。此外，位於 Kurortne 村莊的一個據信用於儲存更多導彈硬體機庫也遭到襲擊。這些行動是烏克蘭旨在削弱俄羅斯對烏克蘭城市進行導彈襲擊能力的更廣泛行動的一部分。[ref:2]

作為大規模報復回應，俄羅斯在週五夜間和整個週六發動了今年最大規模的協調空中襲擊之一。據烏克蘭總統澤連斯基稱，俄羅斯軍隊部署了約 430 架無人機和 68 枚各類導彈，包括 Zircon 反艦導彈和 Iskander-M 彈道導彈。主要目標是基輔首都周邊的能源基礎設施，但在蘇梅、扎波羅熱和哈爾科夫地區也記錄了襲擊。這次襲擊導致烏克蘭各地至少五人死亡，數十人受傷，造成大範圍停電以及住宅樓和學校的嚴重損壞。[ref:5, 1]

此次升級發生在克里姆林宮與西方國家之間緊張的外交爭執之中。3 月 13 日和 14 日，俄羅斯外交部傳喚了英國和法國大使，就使用西方供應的遠程武器襲擊俄羅斯領土提出正式抗議，特別是提到了此前在布良斯克地區造成七人死亡的襲擊事件。莫斯科指責北約專家提供了此類精確打擊所需的直接情報和技術支持，並警告如果這類行動持續下去，將會達到 '根本上新的' 毀滅水平。隨著國際注意力因中東地區的持續衝突而分散，莫斯科和基輔似乎都在加緊其深遠打擊能力，以爭奪戰略主動權。[ref:null]

timeline.title: Moscow Summons Western Ambassadors Following Deadly Ukrainian Missile Strikes on Strategic Russian Infrastructure
timeline.title_zh: 莫斯科就烏克蘭襲擊俄羅斯戰略基礎設施事件傳召西方大使
timeline.sub_title: Russia issued a formal diplomatic protest to the United Kingdom and France after Storm Shadow missiles struck Bryansk, causing dozens of casualties and targeting key microelectronic and energy facilities.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 風暴陰影導彈襲擊了布良斯克，造成數十人傷亡並針對關鍵的微電子和能源設施，俄羅斯向英國和法國發出了正式外交抗議。
timeline.summary:
The Kremlin significantly escalated its diplomatic rhetoric on Friday, March 13, 2026, by summoning the ambassadors of the United Kingdom and France to the Foreign Ministry in Moscow. The summons was issued as a formal protest against the alleged involvement of Western specialists in a deadly Ukrainian missile strike on the Russian city of Bryansk earlier this week. [ref:5, 4, 1] Russian officials claim that the attack, which utilized sophisticated Storm Shadow long-range missiles, resulted in at least seven deaths and left more than 40 others injured, including children. [ref:2] The strike targeted a busy commercial area near the Bryansk State Engineering and Technology University and the Kremniy El microchip plant, one of Russia’s largest manufacturers of microelectronics for military use.

Ukrainian officials have acknowledged responsibility for the Bryansk operation, characterizing it as a justified response to ongoing Russian aggression and a necessary measure to degrade the industrial base supporting Moscow's war effort. President Volodymyr Zelensky defended the use of long-range capabilities, stating that hitting the sources of Russian missile production is essential for protecting Ukrainian civilians. This exchange highlights the deepening involvement of Western-supplied precision weaponry in strikes far behind the established front lines, a development that Moscow has labeled as a deliberate provocation and an act of state-sponsored terrorism.

Parallel to the diplomatic fallout, Ukraine’s deep-strike campaign continued to intensify within the past 24 hours. Overnight between March 12 and March 13, a wave of Ukrainian drones successfully targeted the Tikhoretsk oil depot in the Krasnodar Krai region. Satellite [ref:3] imagery and local reports confirmed that at least two large fuel storage tanks were destroyed in the attack, sparking massive fires at what is considered one of the most critical oil transshipment hubs in southern Russia. This strike [ref:3] follows a reported massive fire at a metallurgy plant in the Tula Oblast, further signaling a coordinated effort by Kyiv to paralyze Russian logistics and energy infrastructure.

The increasing frequency and success of these operations are partly attributed to the maturation of Ukraine’s domestic long-range programs. Reports indicate that the newly developed 'Flamingo' cruise missile, with a range exceeding 1,000 kilometers, is now being regularly integrated into strike packages alongside Western systems. These domestic assets allow Ukraine to circumvent some of the restrictive conditions previously placed on the use of foreign weapons for strikes inside Russian territory. By combining indigenous cruise missiles with high-endurance drones, Ukrainian forces have forced Russia to relocate air defense assets from the front lines to protect internal industrial centers.

The situation remains highly volatile as the international community watches for a potential Russian retaliation. The Russian Foreign Ministry warned that if European capitals continue to facilitate these strikes, they will bear full responsibility for the 'destructive consequences' and any further escalation of the conflict. Meanwhile, Kyiv [ref:5, 1] appears committed to expanding its strategic reach, with defense officials hinting at upcoming operations targeting even more distant production facilities. As the war of attrition moves deeper into the Russian interior, the risk of a direct confrontation between NATO powers and Russia continues to mount.
timeline.summary_zh:
克里姆林宮於 2026 年 3 月 13 日星期五大幅升級了其外交言辭，傳召英國和法國大使到莫斯科外交部。傳召是針對烏克蘭本週早些時候對俄羅斯城市布良斯克發動的致命導彈襲擊，據稱西方專家參與其中，發出了正式抗議。俄羅斯官員聲稱，此次襲擊使用了先進的風暴陰影遠程導彈，造成至少七人死亡，四十多人受傷，其中包括兒童。襲擊目標是布良斯克國立工程技術大學和俄羅斯最大的微電子產品製造商之一克里姆尼埃爾（Kremniy El）微芯片廠附近的一個繁忙商業區。[ref:5, 4, 1, 2]

烏克蘭官員已承認對布良斯克行動負責，認為這是對持續的俄羅斯侵略的合理回應，也是削弱莫斯科戰爭支援工業基礎的必要措施。總統沃洛德米爾·澤倫斯基（Volodymyr Zelensky）捍衛了遠程能力的使用，表示打擊俄羅斯導彈生產的來源對於保護烏克蘭平民至關重要。這次事件凸顯了西方供應的精確武器越來越深入地參與到遠離既定前線的襲擊中，莫斯科已將此舉標籤為蓄意挑釁和國家贊助的恐怖主義行為。

與外交影響同時發生的是，烏克蘭的遠程打擊行動在過去 24 小時內持續加劇。在 3 月 12 日至 13 日的夜間，一批烏克蘭無人機成功襲擊了克拉斯諾達爾邊疆區的季霍列茨克（Tikhoretsk）石油儲備庫。衛星圖像和當地報告證實，襲擊中至少有兩個大型燃料儲存罐被摧毀，引發了當時被認為是俄羅斯南部最關鍵的石油轉運樞紐之一的大火。此次襲擊發生在圖拉州一家冶金廠發生大規模火災之後，進一步表明基輔正在採取協調一致的行動，以癱瘓俄羅斯的物流和能源基礎設施。[ref:3]

這些行動日益頻繁且成功，部分歸因於烏克蘭國內遠程計劃的成熟。報告顯示，新開發的「火烈鳥」（Flamingo）巡航導彈，射程超過 1000 公里，現已與西方系統一同被定期整合到打擊編隊中。這些國內資產使烏克蘭能夠繞過先前對使用外國武器襲擊俄羅斯領土內部施加的一些限制條件。通過結合國產巡航導彈和高續航力無人機，烏克蘭軍隊迫使俄羅斯將防空資產從前線轉移，以保護其國內工業中心。

局勢依然極不穩定，國際社會正密切關注俄羅斯的潛在報復行動。俄羅斯外交部警告說，如果歐洲各國首都繼續縱容這些襲擊，他們將對「破壞性後果」及衝突的任何進一步升級承擔全部責任。同時，基輔似乎決心擴大其戰略打擊範圍，國防官員暗示即將進行針對更遠生產設施的行動。隨著消耗戰深入俄羅斯腹地，北約國家與俄羅斯之間發生直接對抗的風險持續增加。[ref:5, 1]

timeline.title: Ukraine Intensifies Long-Range Missile Strikes on Russian Industrial and Energy Hubs, Inflicting Heavy Casualties
timeline.title_zh: 烏克蘭「風暴陰影」導彈襲擊俄羅斯戰略微芯片廠，造成七死數十傷
timeline.sub_title: Kyiv deployed Storm Shadow missiles to devastate a critical microelectronics plant in Bryansk and launched drone strikes on a major Krasnodar oil hub, marking a strategic escalation.
timeline.sub_title_zh: 一枚高精度遠程導彈襲擊布良斯克「克列姆尼伊·埃爾」工廠，引發俄羅斯重大傷亡，並促使莫斯科威脅軍事升級。
timeline.summary:
Ukrainian forces have significantly intensified their deep-strike campaign within Russian territory, executing a high-precision missile attack on the Kremniy El microelectronics factory in Bryansk. The operation, which utilized British-supplied Storm Shadow cruise missiles launched from modified Su-24M fighters, targeted one of Russia’s most critical semiconductor producers. This facility is a vital link in the Kremlin's defense industrial base, manufacturing essential components and microchips for advanced weaponry, including Iskander and Kh-series cruise missiles. Ukrainian military officials confirmed that the strike achieved significant damage to production workshops, with analysts suggesting the destruction could cripple Russia’s ability to replenish its precision-guided munition stockpiles for months.

The human toll of the Bryansk strike has become clearer over the last 24 hours as rescue operations continue. Russian regional authorities confirmed that the death toll has risen to seven following the discovery of additional victims in the debris. Furthermore, at least 42 individuals were reported injured, some critically, with the total number of casualties reaching nearly 50. Social media footage and satellite imagery from the scene show extensive damage to the industrial complex and nearby civilian areas, including apartment buildings. Ukrainian intelligence indicated that the strike's effectiveness was bolstered by the use of real-time drone surveillance for fire correction, a tactical evolution that allowed for maximum impact with a limited number of cruise missiles.

The Kremlin has reacted with sharp condemnation, characterizing the assault as an act of terrorism intended to derail potential peace negotiations. Spokesman Dmitry Peskov explicitly linked the success of the operation to Western assistance, asserting that the deployment of Storm Shadow missiles would not have been possible without the direct involvement of British technical specialists. Moscow has used this development to reinforce its narrative for the ongoing military campaign, claiming that the "demilitarization" of Ukraine is an absolute necessity to prevent future strikes on Russian soil. Russian Foreign [ref:2] Ministry officials further accused London of being a full party to the conflict and escalating the war to a more lethal level.

Simultaneous with the missile strikes in the west, Ukrainian security services launched a massive drone offensive against energy infrastructure in southern Russia. A major strike on the Tikhoretsk oil pumping station in Krasnodar Krai resulted in massive fires that burned throughout the day. The Tikhoretsk hub serves as a vital logistics artery for the transport of petroleum products to the port of Novorossiysk. Ukrainian Security [ref:1] Service sources described the operation as a significant blow to Russian fuel logistics, aimed at disrupting the supply chains that sustain frontline forces while limiting the Kremlin’s oil export revenues.

This escalation comes as Ukraine secures further international commitments for its autonomous strike capabilities. President Volodymyr Zelensky announced a strategic partnership with Romania to co-produce drones, following a similar move by German partners to deliver advanced air defense interceptors. Kyiv’s strategy now focuses on a multi-layered approach: protecting its own skies while projecting power deep into Russian territory to systematically degrade the enemy's military-economic potential. The use of sophisticated Western hardware for high-impact strikes inside Russia represents a definitive new phase of the conflict, as both sides brace for further intensification of the aerial campaign.
timeline.summary_zh:
烏克蘭部隊已加劇其在俄羅斯境內的遠程襲擊行動，成功鎖定布良斯克市一家關鍵的微電子工廠。此次襲擊發生於3月10日，在過去24小時內被廣泛報導，使用了英國提供的「風暴陰影」巡航導彈摧毀了「克列姆尼伊·埃爾」工廠。該設施是俄羅斯最大的軍用級微芯片和半導體元件生產商之一，這些元件對俄羅斯經常用於襲擊烏克蘭城市中心的導彈的制導系統至關重要。衛星圖像證實，至少有五枚導彈擊中主要生產樓，造成毀滅性損壞及次生爆炸，燃燒了數小時。[ref:1, 2]

據俄羅斯外交部稱，此次精準打擊造成重大平民傷亡，當地官員報告稱，員工和附近居民中有七人死亡，四十二人受傷。儘管烏克蘭官員尚未證實具體的傷亡數字，但烏克蘭總參謀部將此次行動定性為成功削弱了俄羅斯的國防工業基礎。值得注意的是，此次任務標誌著烏克蘭無人系統部隊的戰術演進，他們部署了一架專門的無人機來提供實時火力修正。這使得導彈操作員能夠在飛行中調整目標數據，確保以相對較少的彈藥對工廠的生產基礎設施產生最大影響。[ref:1]

克里姆林宮對此事件的反應是強烈譴責，並警告將對西方國家採取直接升級行動。俄羅斯外交部長謝爾蓋·拉夫羅夫及外交部發言人聲稱，若沒有北約人員直接參與目標設定和飛行規劃，就不可能使用「風暴陰影」導彈。莫斯科將此次事件描述為進入衝突一個根本上更危險的階段，並威脅將採取可能涉及新一級別破壞的對策。儘管有這些威脅，俄羅斯極端民族主義軍事博主批評國防部未能保護如此深處俄羅斯領空的戰略資產。

除了布良斯克襲擊外，烏克蘭還在邊境地區保持高強度的空中行動。過去一天，據報導索契發生了大規模的24小時無人機襲擊，目標是能源基礎設施和後勤樞紐。俄羅斯防空部隊聲稱在多個地區攔截了185架無人機，包括被佔領的克里米亞和黑海。與此同時，有報導稱對與主要黑海天然氣管道相關的壓縮站進行了襲擊嘗試，這表明烏克蘭正致力於同時削弱俄羅斯的軍事製造、能源出口和區域防空網絡。隨著局勢發展，焦點仍將是俄羅斯是否會兌現其報復威脅，還是會將重點轉向保護其剩餘的國防工業設施。
